meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome no project ideas. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first real app.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis is a rite of passage for every developer. The solution is not to wait for a stroke of genius, but to build a modular SaaS starter kit. By mastering core authentication, database, and payment scaffolding, you transform abstract ideas into rapid, repeatable, and profitable prototypes.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking like a metronome counting down your wasted potential. You have the skills, but the chasm between 'learning to code' and 'building a product' feels like an infinite void. This isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of framework.

Today, we stop building 'todo lists' and start building infrastructure. By leveraging a SaaS starter kit, you are no longer just a student writing code—you are an architect laying the foundation for a scalable business. We will use Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your setup phase into a high-velocity launchpad, effectively killing the blank page syndrome forever.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Auth Flow
steps.description: Establish a secure authentication foundation. This ensures your app is ready for users from day one, teaching you the fundamentals of session management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular Next.js authentication boilerplate using NextAuth.js, focusing on email/password and Google OAuth providers with TypeScript types.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize a secure NextAuth setup with JWT sessions in the current directory.'

steps.title: Database Schema Modeling
steps.description: Define a robust PostgreSQL schema. Understanding data relationships is what separates a script from a real-world SaaS application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a SaaS platform with User, Subscription, and Project models, including one-to-many relationships.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Setup Prisma schema with PostgreSQL and generate the migrations.'

steps.title: Integrating Payment Infrastructure
steps.description: Connect Stripe to handle subscriptions. This is the heartbeat of any SaaS and a critical skill for any developer building for the market.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe integration service that handles webhook events for subscription creation and cancellation for a Next.js API route.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement Stripe checkout session logic and webhook handler.'

steps.title: Building the Dashboard Interface
steps.description: Construct a responsive dashboard using Tailwind CSS. A clean UI gives your users confidence in your solution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a responsive dashboard layout with a sidebar, top navigation, and a main content area using Tailwind CSS and Lucide icons.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Scaffold a dashboard layout component using Tailwind CSS.'

steps.title: Deploying the MVP
steps.description: Ship your code to production. Seeing your app live on a custom domain is the final milestone in your journey as a builder.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a step-by-step CI/CD configuration guide for deploying the current Next.js project to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure Vercel deployment settings and environment variables.'

pro_tips: Always build for 'multi-tenancy' from day one; it forces you to design better data architecture.
Prioritize 'feature flags' so you can ship broken code to production without breaking the app for users.
Use 'Environment Variables' for everything; never hardcode a secret, even in your local prototype.
Focus on 'Time to First Dollar' over 'Perfect Code'; your first users will give you better feedback than any tutorial.
Log everything; observability is the only way to debug an app once it leaves your local machine.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is functional, build three distinct 'micro-tools' on top of it (e.g., an AI text summarizer, a lead tracker, or a task scheduler) to prove the modularity of your system.
content.conversion_block: Stop dreaming about the 'perfect idea' and start building the infrastructure that makes every idea possible. You have the tools, the guide, and the momentum to move from student to founder in a single weekend. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
